title: podman failing with ERRO[0000]  error validating containers config: log size max should be negative or >= 8192
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Any podman .. command under a certain user gives error.
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

just run:
```
 $ podman 
ERRO[0000]  error validating containers config: log size max should be negative or >= 8192 
```

All is ok under a different user or different machine.


**Describe the results you received:**
```
ERRO[0000]  error validating containers config: log size max should be negative or >= 8192 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Anything else.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This happens all of the time but only on one of my machines. Guess I have to delete something somewhere, but what?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
ERRO[0000]  error validating containers config: log size max should be negative or >= 8192 

$ su - test -c 'podman version'
Password: 
Version:      2.0.6
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.14
Built:        Tue Sep  1 21:27:59 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64



```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug
ERRO[0000]  error validating containers config: log size max should be negative or >= 8192 
$ su - test -c 'podman info --debug'

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

    path: /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock

  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 1
  swapFree: 16570118144
  swapTotal: 16609439744
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.6-1.fc31.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

have googled and checked the guide. did not find a solution

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical box

comments.body:
Please paste the output of the command
```
cat /home/test/.config/containers/storage.conf
```
Providing `~/.config/containers/containers.conf` and `~/.config.containers/libpod.conf` if they exist would also be very helpful.

Assuming you are interested in the config file for the account that has the issue:

```
$ cat /home/martin/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
  driver = "overlay"
  runroot = "/run/user/1000/containers"
  graphroot = "/home/martin/.local/share/containers/storage"
  [storage.options]
    size = ""
    remap-uids = ""
    remap-gids = ""
    ignore_chown_errors = ""
    remap-user = ""
    remap-group = ""
    skip_mount_home = ""
    mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
    mountopt = ""
    [storage.options.aufs]
      mountopt = ""
    [storage.options.btrfs]
      min_space = ""
      size = ""
    [storage.options.thinpool]
      autoextend_percent = ""
      autoextend_threshold = ""
      basesize = ""
      blocksize = ""
      directlvm_device = ""
      directlvm_device_force = ""
      fs = ""
      log_level = ""
      min_free_space = ""
      mkfsarg = ""
      mountopt = ""
      size = ""
      use_deferred_deletion = ""
      use_deferred_removal = ""
      xfs_nospace_max_retries = ""
    [storage.options.overlay]
      ignore_chown_errors = ""
      mountopt = ""
      mount_program = ""
      size = ""
      skip_mount_home = ""
    [storage.options.vfs]
      ignore_chown_errors = ""
    [storage.options.zfs]
      mountopt = ""
      fsname = ""
      size = ""
```
```
$ cat ~/.config/containers/libpod.conf 
runtime = "/usr/bin/crun"
max_log_size = 0
num_locks = 0
cgroup_check = true
```
For the `test` account (the one that works) the config files don't exist.
I recommend just removing that `~/.config/containers/libpod.conf ` file - that should resolve matters
Seems so, thx. That really helps. I wonder how I got into that situation.
Older versions of podman would create that file in the homedir,  Somehow it got corrupted.
